passage: The Eiffel Tower was originally built as a temporary exhibit for the 1889 World's Fair; later designated a permanent landmark after public opinion shifted in its favor. Today it stands as one of the most recognizable structures in the world and attracts millions of visitors each year.
stem: Which choice best maintains sentence boundaries?

choices.label: B
choices.text: World's Fair, later designated a permanent landmark after public opinion shifted in its favor.

choices.label: C
choices.text: World's Fair. Later designated a permanent landmark after public opinion shifted in its favor.

choices.label: D
choices.text: World's Fair; and later designated a permanent landmark after public opinion shifted in its favor.

explanation: The phrase 'later designated a permanent landmark after public opinion shifted in its favor' is a participial phrase modifying 'The Eiffel Tower.' It has no subject and no finite verb of its own, so it is NOT an independent clause. A semicolon (Choice A) can only join two independent clauses, making it incorrect here. A comma (Choice B) correctly attaches the participial phrase to the preceding independent clause 'The Eiffel Tower was originally built as a temporary exhibit for the 1889 World's Fair.' Choice B is the only grammatically correct option.

distractorRationale.A: NO CHANGE uses a semicolon before 'later designated a permanent landmark,' but a semicolon must join two independent clauses. The phrase 'later designated a permanent landmark after public opinion shifted in its favor' is a participial phrase with no subject and no finite verb, so the semicolon is incorrect here.
distractorRationale.C: Placing a period before 'Later designated a permanent landmark after public opinion shifted in its favor' creates a fragment. This phrase has no subject and no finite verb, so it cannot stand alone as a complete sentence.
distractorRationale.D: Pairing a semicolon with a coordinating conjunction ('and') is a common student error. Even setting that aside, 'later designated a permanent landmark after public opinion shifted in its favor' remains a participial phrase without a subject or finite verb, so neither a semicolon nor a conjunction can correctly introduce it as a second independent clause.
